title: Four Banks, One Fish Tale
summary: Fen claims the millpond carp favor whichever bank he is not sitting on. He needs Kern to watch equal stretches of shore before moving the tiny chair reserved for his most persuasive fish.

steps.objective: Watch for fish rings at four marked stretches around the millpond for twenty counts each
steps.hint: Same amount of watching at every spot, or Fen will blame the chair.

steps.objective: Cast Fen's reed lure once from each marked stretch and note where the fish answer
steps.hint: One enormous splash is exciting. Four ordinary splashes may tell you more.

steps.objective: Return to Fen and compare all four stretches before choosing the liveliest bank
steps.hint: Bit votes for the bank with the fewest opinions about golden lights.

title: Ribbons in the Wrong Rows
summary: A gust scrambled Tansy's route ribbons at the hives. Rather than trust the loudest bees, she asks Kern to watch every row, sort the ribbons by flight path, and change them one at a time.

steps.objective: Watch one full bee flight cycle at each of the three hive rows
steps.hint: Sunny, shady, and judgmental bees all deserve an equal look.

steps.objective: Match each loose route ribbon to the row whose bees follow its flower path
steps.hint: Follow where they return from, not merely where the busiest cloud begins.

steps.objective: Tie one ribbon in place, wait for the bees to follow it, then secure the other two
steps.hint: Test one change first. Ten thousand sisters can make ten thousand corrections.

title: The Gate That Cried Goose
summary: The eastern gate bell keeps sounding for harmless traffic. Rowan wants fewer false alarms without ignoring real trouble, so Kern must test sheep, cart, and goose crossings separately.

steps.objective: Inspect the bell cord and mark three clear crossing lines at Bootstrap's eastern gate
steps.hint: A tidy test begins with tidy chalk. Rowan has very strong feelings about both.

steps.objective: Observe one sheep crossing, one handcart crossing, and one goose crossing separately
steps.hint: Change only the traveler. If everything moves at once, every suspect gets an alibi.

steps.objective: Adjust the loose lower cord loop disturbed by the goose crossing, then repeat all three crossings
steps.hint: Quiet for geese is good. Quiet for every visitor would be a rather poor alarm.

steps.objective: Report the before-and-after results to Rowan Threshold
steps.hint: Describe what happened precisely. Leave out the goose's opinion of the badge.

title: Sir Nearest Counts Sheep
summary: Six sheep have wandered between the pasture flocks, and Tilly's broomstick insists the closest flock is always correct. Kern must use more than distance before Sir Nearest earns a shepherd's hat.

steps.objective: Meet Tilly and Sir Nearest beside the six wandering sheep in the pastures
steps.hint: The broomstick is the one already wearing a grass whistle.

steps.objective: Check each sheep's ear-ribbon shape, bell tone, and wool curl
steps.hint: One clue can fool you. Two agreeing clues are much less woolly.

steps.objective: Guide each wandering sheep to the flock sharing at least two of its three signs
steps.hint: The nearest flock is a good first guess, not a royal command from a broomstick.

steps.objective: Watch the flocks settle, correct any mismatch, and report to Tilly
steps.hint: A calm flock is useful evidence. So is one sheep immediately leaving.

title: The Blue Marble Standard
summary: One irrigation branch reaches the sheep pastures late, but Jory refuses to dig on a hunch. Time his blue marble through every ditch, make one small correction, and test the water again.

steps.objective: Walk the three irrigation branches to the sheep pastures without clearing anything yet
steps.hint: First see the ditches as they are. Mud dislikes witnesses but cannot stop them.

steps.objective: Release Jory's blue marble at each branch and count until it reaches the pasture marker
steps.hint: Start from the same stone each time, unless you enjoy arguments with a ditch keeper.

steps.objective: Remove the smallest root-and-stone blockage from the slowest branch
steps.hint: One careful change beats one heroic trench. Jory may have mentioned this.

steps.objective: Repeat all three marble runs and confirm no branch now floods or stalls
steps.hint: A faster marble is promising. Three healthy flows are the real answer.

title: The Missing Ledger Pages: Flour
summary: Pages are missing from Elowen's oldest seed ledger. A dusting of flour on the torn binding points toward the mill, but she wants matching evidence before anyone blames the wind.

steps.objective: Examine the torn binding, then note its page size, thread color, and flour smudge
steps.hint: Elowen says color alone is unreliable. Three details make a kinder argument.

steps.objective: Recover three matching scraps around the mill, order them by harvest tally, and return them to Elowen
steps.hint: Look beside the grain sacks, under the steps, and where the mill breeze escapes.

title: The Missing Ledger Pages: Downstream
summary: The recovered scraps end mid-tally and carry millpond reeds. Elowen asks Kern to follow the water's route through the irrigation ditches and check every likely snag, not just the first one.

steps.objective: Follow the irrigation ditch from the millpond, inspect four spaced snag points, and recover three damp ledger pages
steps.hint: The nearest reeds are tempting. The farthest reeds know this and feel neglected.

steps.objective: Order the pages by their overlapping harvest tallies and bring the sequence to Elowen
steps.hint: If the middle page fits both neighbors, you probably have more than a lucky guess.

title: The Missing Ledger Pages: The Ruins' Edge
summary: The restored tally ends with a sketched delivery route to the Seed Vault ruins. Elowen wants an outside-only comparison, the final page if it remains—and absolutely no brave improvising.

steps.objective: Follow the sketched route to the Seed Vault ruins' edge and find the final page without crossing the boundary stones
steps.hint: Elowen underlined 'edge' three times. Bit sees no reason to improvise a dungeon.

steps.objective: Return to Elowen and report the page and a distant purple flicker as observations, not conclusions
steps.hint: Facts may be quieter than guesses. Elowen is listening for both.
